prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
# Done

The last period drops like a stone into water,
and something in my shoulders finally remembers
how to fall.

Weeks of it — the circling, the second-guessing,
the 2 a.m. arguments with myself
over whether any of it was worth
the particular wreckage of my kitchen table.

And now the strange lightness.
The way I don't know what to do with my hands.
The folder closed, the thing sent out into the world
to become whatever the world decides it is.

I stand at the window.
A bus goes by. Someone is walking a dog.
Everything ordinary, and somehow
I have never been so glad of it.
